mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains at the zero lower bound, equity discount rates stay compressed and risk appetite improves, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive. The signal fails if rate cuts coincide with recessionary demand collapse or if the market already prices the easing before the observation date.
failure_condition: Rejected if FEDFUNDS declines or stays near zero and triggered 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and slower hiring, which should depress earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect fails when unemployment rises alongside strong productivity or when markets treat the labor deterioration as a temporary lagging indicator.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of cases after the unemployment threshold is crossed, or if conditional Rank IC is not negative over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals broadening labor income and stronger retail demand, which should lift near-term earnings revisions and support S&P 500 returns. The mechanism fails if job gains are concentrated in low-wage sectors or if sales strength is inventory-driven rather than final-demand driven.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint increases in PAYEMS and RSAFS do not outperform single-series cases, or if triggered 20-day SPX returns are not above the unconditional baseline by at least 2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO relative to prior months signals stronger industrial output and improved capacity utilization, which should be followed by positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal fails if production is boosted only by inventory rebuilds or if output gains are offset by margin compression from cost shocks.
failure_condition: Rejected if consecutive INDPRO gains are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or if the sign of the conditional effect is not positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in HOUST indicates improving residential investment and downstream demand for materials, labor, and durable goods, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect fails when housing starts rise because of speculative overbuilding or when mortgage-rate pressure overwhelms activity.
failure_condition: Rejected if positive HOUST trends do not precede above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the conditional return difference is indistinguishable from zero across any 4-quarter rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, labor market deterioration is broad and not just a participation artifact, so earnings expectations should weaken and S&P 500 returns over the next month should be negative. The mechanism fails if payroll softness is offset by falling inflation or if unemployment rises due to labor-force reentry without actual job losses.
failure_condition: Rejected if the combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in a majority of instances, or if the interaction term is not more negative than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation slowdown paired with low FEDFUNDS implies real policy is accommodative, so equity multiples should expand and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if disinflation comes from demand collapse rather than benign supply normalization.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend falls for 3 months while FEDFUNDS stays below 1%
failure_condition: Rejected if low-rate disinflation episodes do not deliver positive 20-day SPX returns above baseline, or if the joint signal loses significance once CPIAUCSL changes are controlled for separately.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand disappointment, defined by weaker GDPC1 and softer RSAFS, should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because revenue growth and earnings expectations deteriorate together. The mechanism fails if GDP revisions erase the weakness or if retail sales are temporarily weak due to seasonal distortions.
failure_condition: Rejected if simultaneous weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional return spread versus baseline is not negative over any 3-year rolling window.
